description: The Communist International was a magazine covering the work of Communist International. It was established in 1919 and published monthly by Workers Library Publishers until 1940. The magazine servied as a crucial organ of the Communist International and Workers’ movement, providing analytical articles, propaganda, and strategic guidance for revolutionary workers worldwide. The periodicals contained diverse articles, including reports on revolutionary events, theoretical analyses, and mass agitation materials. Subjects covered ranged from the lessons of the October Revolution, issues related to trade union organization and tactics, the fight against fascism, and analysis of capitalist development in various countries. It aimed to educate workers and party members on the importance of Soviet Power, the tactics of the revolutionary proletariat, and the necessity of international solidarity among socialist forces. Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communist_International_(magazine)
subject: Political Science
title: The Communist International
